members.name: CellularDistanceFunction
members.summary: Determines how the distance to the nearest/second-nearest point is computed.

members.name: CellularJitter
members.summary: Maximum distance a point can move off of its grid position. Set to [code]0[/code] for an even grid.

members.name: CellularReturnType
members.summary: Return type from cellular noise calculations.

members.name: DomainWarpAmplitude
members.summary: Sets the maximum warp distance from the origin.

members.name: DomainWarpEnabled
members.summary: If enabled, another FastNoiseLite instance is used to warp the space, resulting in a distortion of the noise.

members.name: DomainWarpFractalGain
members.summary: Determines the strength of each subsequent layer of the noise which is used to warp the space. A low value places more emphasis on the lower frequency base layers, while a high value puts more emphasis on the higher frequency layers.

members.name: DomainWarpFractalLacunarity
members.summary: The change in frequency between octaves, also known as "lacunarity", of the fractal noise which warps the space. Increasing this value results in higher octaves, producing noise with finer details and a rougher appearance.

members.name: DomainWarpFractalOctaves
members.summary: The number of noise layers that are sampled to get the final value for the fractal noise which warps the space.

members.name: DomainWarpFractalType
members.summary: The method for combining octaves into a fractal which is used to warp the space.

members.name: DomainWarpFrequency
members.summary: Frequency of the noise which warps the space. Low frequency results in smooth noise while high frequency results in rougher, more granular noise.

members.name: DomainWarpType
members.summary: The warp algorithm.

members.name: FractalGain
members.summary: Determines the strength of each subsequent layer of noise in fractal noise. A low value places more emphasis on the lower frequency base layers, while a high value puts more emphasis on the higher frequency layers.

members.name: FractalLacunarity
members.summary: Frequency multiplier between subsequent octaves. Increasing this value results in higher octaves producing noise with finer details and a rougher appearance.

members.name: FractalOctaves
members.summary: The number of noise layers that are sampled to get the final value for fractal noise types.

members.name: FractalPingPongStrength
members.summary: Sets the strength of the fractal ping pong type.

members.name: FractalType
members.summary: The method for combining octaves into a fractal.

members.name: FractalWeightedStrength
members.summary: Higher weighting means higher octaves have less impact if lower octaves have a large impact.

members.name: Frequency
members.summary: The frequency for all noise types. Low frequency results in smooth noise while high frequency results in rougher, more granular noise.

members.name: NoiseType
members.summary: The noise algorithm used.

members.name: Offset
members.summary: Translate the noise input coordinates by the given [Vector3].

members.name: Seed
members.summary: The random number seed for all noise types.

values.name: DISTANCE_EUCLIDEAN
values.summary: Euclidean distance to the nearest point.

values.name: DISTANCE_EUCLIDEAN_SQUARED
values.summary: Squared Euclidean distance to the nearest point.

values.name: DISTANCE_HYBRID
values.summary: Blend of [constant DISTANCE_EUCLIDEAN] and [constant DISTANCE_MANHATTAN] to give curved cell boundaries.

values.name: DISTANCE_MANHATTAN
values.summary: Manhattan distance (taxicab metric) to the nearest point.

values.name: RETURN_CELL_VALUE
values.summary: The cellular distance function will return the same value for all points within a cell.

values.name: RETURN_DISTANCE
values.summary: The cellular distance function will return a value determined by the distance to the nearest point.

values.name: RETURN_DISTANCE2
values.summary: The cellular distance function returns the distance to the second-nearest point.

values.name: RETURN_DISTANCE2_ADD
values.summary: The distance to the nearest point is added to the distance to the second-nearest point.

values.name: RETURN_DISTANCE2_DIV
values.summary: The distance to the nearest point is divided by the distance to the second-nearest point.

values.name: RETURN_DISTANCE2_MUL
values.summary: The distance to the nearest point is multiplied with the distance to the second-nearest point.

values.name: RETURN_DISTANCE2_SUB
values.summary: The distance to the nearest point is subtracted from the distance to the second-nearest point.

values.name: DOMAIN_WARP_FRACTAL_INDEPENDENT
values.summary: Warping the space independently for each octave, resulting in a more chaotic distortion.

values.name: DOMAIN_WARP_FRACTAL_NONE
values.summary: No fractal noise for warping the space.

values.name: DOMAIN_WARP_FRACTAL_PROGRESSIVE
values.summary: Warping the space progressively, octave for octave, resulting in a more "liquified" distortion.

values.name: DOMAIN_WARP_BASIC_GRID
values.summary: The domain is warped using a simple noise grid (not as smooth as the other methods, but more performant).

values.name: DOMAIN_WARP_SIMPLEX
values.summary: The domain is warped using the simplex noise algorithm.

values.name: DOMAIN_WARP_SIMPLEX_REDUCED
values.summary: The domain is warped using a simplified version of the simplex noise algorithm.

values.name: FRACTAL_FBM
values.summary: Method using Fractional Brownian Motion to combine octaves into a fractal.

values.name: FRACTAL_NONE
values.summary: No fractal noise.

values.name: FRACTAL_PING_PONG
values.summary: Method of combining octaves into a fractal with a ping pong effect.

values.name: FRACTAL_RIDGED
values.summary: Method of combining octaves into a fractal resulting in a "ridged" look.

values.name: TYPE_CELLULAR
values.summary: Cellular includes both Worley noise and Voronoi diagrams which creates various regions of the same value.

values.name: TYPE_PERLIN
values.summary: A lattice of random gradients. Their dot products are interpolated to obtain values in between the lattices.

values.name: TYPE_SIMPLEX
values.summary: As opposed to [constant TYPE_PERLIN], gradients exist in a simplex lattice rather than a grid lattice, avoiding directional artifacts. Internally uses FastNoiseLite's OpenSimplex2 noise type.

values.name: TYPE_SIMPLEX_SMOOTH
values.summary: Modified, higher quality version of [constant TYPE_SIMPLEX], but slower. Internally uses FastNoiseLite's OpenSimplex2S noise type.

values.name: TYPE_VALUE
values.summary: A lattice of points are assigned random values then interpolated based on neighboring values.

values.name: TYPE_VALUE_CUBIC
values.summary: Similar to value noise ([constant TYPE_VALUE]), but slower. Has more variance in peaks and valleys. Cubic noise can be used to avoid certain artifacts when using value noise to create a bumpmap. In general, you should always use this mode if the value noise is being used for a heightmap or bumpmap.

constants.name: TYPE_VALUE
constants.summary: A lattice of points are assigned random values then interpolated based on neighboring values.

constants.name: TYPE_VALUE_CUBIC
constants.summary: Similar to value noise ([constant TYPE_VALUE]), but slower. Has more variance in peaks and valleys. Cubic noise can be used to avoid certain artifacts when using value noise to create a bumpmap. In general, you should always use this mode if the value noise is being used for a heightmap or bumpmap.
